chunks.title: Importance of Fasting Insulin and Prolactin Tests
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses the significance of fasting insulin and prolactin levels in blood tests, emphasizing that these tests are often overlooked by healthcare providers. They share their personal experience with fluctuating insulin and prolactin levels and stress the importance of repeating tests for accurate results.
chunks.transcript: let's start with two blood tests that I think are some of the most important that you can do that are very rarely done by mainstream Physicians this is the fasting insulin and the prolactin and as you can see here I am a 45 year old male I was born June 29 1977. that's me this is my blood work so my insulin My fasting insulin was 3.2 micro IU per ML on this blood work which was in mid-march 2023 and my prolactin was 10.5 now for context usually almost every single time that I've done fasting insulin it's right around three micro IU per ml or lower but I did have previous blood work on the 6th of March which is why I repeated this one where I had a 5.8 out of nowhere so I don't know why my fasting insulin was up on the 6th of March I don't really vary my diet that much but this is just to say that if your blood work looks a little funny just repeat it sometimes things are strange in what we're doing the body is complex and we can't always go based on one snapshot I'll talk about that with regard to lipids later in this podcast as well so this is a outlying value for me a 5.8 is it a lab error was I just more tired I was traveling for all of this blood work so it's influenced by that I'm not sleeping in the same bed that I'm used to in Costa Rica which is where I spend most of my time and I'm frankly not my best self when I'm traveling because I'm not in my space I'm exposed to lice at Night My circadian rhythm is off and moving across time zones so take that into consideration when we're looking at these Labs but regardless um one of these fasting insulin levels was not horrible 5.8 is not horrible but if somebody came to me with a fasting incident 5.9 it's a it's a little higher than I'd like to see it so I repeated it 3.2 which is more like what I'm used to seeing you'll see here my prolactin was 11.8 on the 6th of March again I think that on the 6th of March I just really hadn't slept in well the night before prolactin tends to be a little bit elevated when you haven't slept well when you are fatigued and even this 10.5 from this mid-march blood work so I got blood work on the 6th of March and probably on the 15th or something of March and this 10.5 for blackton is also high for me I've had prolactins in the sevens before which is probably more of my normal why do I care about prolactin prolactin is important when you're thinking about dopamine and when you're thinking about testosterone I had a friend recently who sent me his blood work and his prolactin was 34 and I thought you need to work that up you need to look and see if you have a little adenoma in your brain that's not a normal level of prolactin in the human body especially for a male or any woman who is not breastfeeding so prolactin is something that can interfere with sex drive with erectile function with weight loss with libido all of these things and so you want to know what your prolactin is and it should kind of track with metabolic Health the thing to know about prolactin also is that it is elevated after an orgasm so if you've had sex or an orgasm the night before you get your blood work it might be a little higher but in metabolically healthy males specifically there should be a quick return to Baseline of prolactin after orgasm I imagine it's the same for women we can look into that data on a future podcast as well so start your blood work with a fasting insulin and a prolactin make sure you get those two know what levels are normal for you you definitely want your prolact and I would say less than 15 which is the reference interval and I would say probably less than 10 and understand like I said that if you are very tired or fatigued or had sex right before this test it might not be a totally accurate measure of things but if you're prolactin is in the 30s or in the 20s or in the High Teens something is off there and you need to dig into that and if your insulin is really much above four or five then you need to think about what is causing insulin resistance and a theme throughout this podcast on blood work

chunks.title: Effects of Prolactin on Health
chunks.summary: The speaker explains how prolactin levels can affect various aspects of health, including libido, weight loss, and metabolic health. They caution that elevated prolactin levels, especially those seen in their friend's blood work, should be investigated further for potential underlying issues.
chunks.transcript: prolactin is important when you're thinking about dopamine and when you're thinking about testosterone I had a friend recently who sent me his blood work and his prolactin was 34 and I thought you need to work that up you need to look and see if you have a little adenoma in your brain that's not a normal level of prolactin in the human body especially for a male or any woman who is not breastfeeding so prolactin is something that can interfere with sex drive with erectile function with weight loss with libido all of these things and so you want to know what your prolactin is and it should kind of track with metabolic Health the thing to know about prolactin also is that it is elevated after an orgasm so if you've had sex or an orgasm the night before you get your blood work it might be a little higher but in metabolically healthy males specifically there should be a quick return to Baseline of prolactin after orgasm I imagine it's the same for women we can look into that data on a future podcast as well so start your blood work with a fasting insulin and a prolactin make sure you get those two know what levels are normal for you you definitely want your prolact and I would say less than 15 which is the reference interval and I would say probably less than 10 and understand like I said that if you are very tired or fatigued or had sex right before this test it might not be a totally accurate measure of things but if you're prolactin is in the 30s or in the 20s or in the High Teens something is off there and you need to dig into

chunks.title: Addressing Elevated Fasting Insulin
chunks.summary: The discussion shifts to managing elevated fasting insulin levels, highlighting cortisol and DHEA ratios as key factors. The speaker suggests limiting certain dietary fats and emphasizes the importance of blood tests to monitor these hormones.
chunks.transcript: taking action on that I'll talk in this podcast about what you can do to take action regarding that Cliff Notes it doesn't have to do with removing sugar from your diet in the form of fruit and honey I think that's actually not a problem for humans though that's a controversial view I've spoken about in the past last week's podcast with Georgie had some information about that as well the major thing I think you need to do two major things you need to do if you have an elevated fasting insulin is think about your Baseline cortisol levels and I'll show you my cortisol to DHEA in one moment and what is raising your cortisol is it leaky God is it gut inflammation causing cortisol to be elevated because of this fragment of gram-negative bacteria called lipopolysaccharide or endotoxin that's moving into your body and limit your consumption your intake of omega-6 primarily but also don't overdo omega-3

chunks.title: Understanding Cortisol and Its Impact
chunks.summary: The speaker elaborates on cortisol levels, sharing their own blood test results and explaining how cortisol relates to stress and overall health. They provide guidance on monitoring cortisol levels and discuss how cortisol may contribute to various health issues, including insulin resistance.
chunks.transcript: is from March the 6th blood work my morning cortisol was 15.8 again previously my morning cortisol was 10.0 this is probably some indication of poor sleep as I'm traveling but my dheas is 126 and previously 139 micrograms per deciliter so this ratio cortisol to dheas is perhaps the single easiest but also most powerful ratio to assess how stressed you are and to potentially assess your longevity or the trajectory that you are headed toward in your longevity what do you want this ratio to be less than 0.3 you can see that even in my stressed state with poor sleep my ratio is 0.124 so I'm not in a bad spot and the ratio was even lower when I was perhaps a little better slept in November 10 over 139 both of these are the same units micrograms per deciliter so understand what your cortisol is get an AM cortis is all these are both am fasting cortisols and if you are really interested in knowing where your cortisol level is get it throughout the day because understanding your cortisol will give you many insights into your overall health and could help you unravel untangle what is causing both non-alcoholic fatty liver disease to foreshadow something I'll talk about later in this podcast and your overall stress and insulin resistance so again let's repeat this if your fasting insulin is elevated limit polyunsaturated fatty acids that is seed oils corn canola sunflower safflower grape seed I talked about this tons on previous podcasts and on my social media and manage your cortisol get that as low as possible stress reduction is improving Your Sleep Quality whatever you need to do think about what's raising your cortisol is it damaged to your gut causing again endotoxin this fragment of gram-negative bacterial cell walls when the gram-negative bacteria proliferate in the colon this seems to

chunks.title: Lifestyle Changes for Managing Hormones
chunks.summary: The speaker outlines lifestyle adjustments necessary for managing hormone levels, particularly focusing on reducing polyunsaturated fatty acids in the diet and improving sleep quality to lower cortisol. They emphasize that awareness of these factors can mitigate health risks associated with hormone imbalances.
